debug_info.goal:
α : Type u_1
β : Type u_2
G✝ H C : Graph α β
S X Y : Set α
M M' : Set β
u v x y z : α
e f : β
P P' : WList α β
hM : @Graph.IsMatching α β G✝ M
G : Graph α β
⊢ ∀ (I : Set α), (∃ M, @Graph.IsMatching α β G M ∧ I ⊆ V(G, M)) → I ⊆ G.vertexSet

failed_attempts:
exact h2 h1
  exact h (hI hx)

intro I hI
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hI'⟩ := hI
    have h : V(G, M) ⊆ G.vertexSet := by
      intro x hx
      exact hx.1
    exact Set.Subset.trans hI' h

intro S hS
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hSM⟩ := hS
    have h₁ : S ⊆ V(G) := by
      have h₂ : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G) := by
        intro x hx
        obtain ⟨e, he⟩ := hx
        exact he.left_mem
      exact Set.Subset.trans hSM h₂
    exact h₁
  <;> simp_all [V_eq_empty_iff]
  <;> aesop

intro S hS
    have h₁ : S ⊆ V(G) := by
      obtain ⟨M, hM, hS⟩ := hS
      have h₂ : S ⊆ V(G, M) := hS
      have h₃ : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G) := by
        intro v hv
        simp_all [VGraphMem]
        <;> aesop
      exact h₂.trans h₃
    exact h₁
  ⊢ ∀ (I : Set α), (∃ M, @Graph.IsMatching α β G M ∧ I ⊆ V(G, M)) → I ⊆ G.vertexSet := by
    intro I hI
    have h₁ : I ⊆ G.vertexSet := by
      obtain ⟨M, hM, hI⟩ := hI
      have h₂ : I ⊆ V(G, M) := hI
      have h₃ : V(G, M) ⊆ G.vertexSet := by
        intro v hv
        simp_all [VGraphMem]
        <;> aesop
      exact h₂.trans h₃
    exact h₁

intro S hS
    have h₁ : ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ S ⊆ V(G, M) := hS
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hS'⟩ := h₁
    have h₂ : S ⊆ V(G) := by
      intro x hx
      have h₃ : x ∈ S := hx
      have h₄ : S ⊆ V(G, M) := hS'
      have h₅ : x ∈ V(G, M) := h₄ h₃
      have h₆ : x ∈ V(G) := by
        have h₇ : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G) := by
          intro y hy
          -- Since M is a subset of E(G), V(G, M) is a subset of V(G)
          have h₈ : e ∈ M → e ∈ E(G) := by
            intro h₈
            exact hM.1 h₈
          simp_all [V_subset_iff]
          <;> aesop
        exact h₇ h₅
      exact h₆
    exact h₂
  ∀ (I : Set α), (∃ M, @Graph.IsMatching α β G M ∧ I ⊆ V(G, M)) → I ⊆ G.vertexSet := by
    intro I hI
    have h₁ : I ⊆ G.vertexSet := by
      -- Extract the matching M and the subset relation from hI
      obtain ⟨M, hM, hI'⟩ := hI
      -- Show that V(G, M) is a subset of G.vertexSet
      have h₂ : V(G, M) ⊆ G.vertexSet := by
        intro x hx
        simp only [Graph.VertexSet.mem_coe, Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hx ⊢
        -- Since M is a subset of E(G), every vertex in V(G, M) is in V(G)
        have h₃ : x ∈ V(G, M) := hx
        have h₄ : x ∈ V(G) := by
          simp_all [Graph.vertexSet, Set.subset_def]
          <;>
          (try aesop) <;>
          (try simp_all [Graph.vertexSet, Set.subset_def]) <;>
          (try aesop)
        exact h₄
      -- Use the subset relation to conclude that I is a subset of G.vertexSet
      have h₃ : I ⊆ V(G, M) := hI'
      have h₄ : V(G, M) ⊆ G.vertexSet := h₂
      exact Set.Subset.trans h₃ h₄
    exact h₁

intro I hI
    have h_main : I ⊆ G.vertexSet := by
      obtain ⟨M, hM, hI⟩ := hI
      have h₁ : V(G, M) ⊆ G.vertexSet := by
        intro x hx
        -- For any x ∈ V(G, M), there exists an edge e ∈ M such that x is an endpoint of e
        have h₂ : x ∈ ⋃ (e : β) (_ : e ∈ M), V(G, e) := hx
        rcases h₂ with ⟨e, he, hx⟩
        -- Since e ∈ M and M ⊆ E(G), e ∈ E(G)
        have h₃ : e ∈ M := he
        have h₄ : M ⊆ E(G) := hM.subset
        have h₅ : e ∈ E(G) := h₄ h₃
        -- The endpoints of e are in G.vertexSet
        have h₆ : V(G, e) ⊆ G.vertexSet := by
          intro y hy
          have h₇ : y ∈ V(G, e) := hy
          have h₈ : y ∈ G.vertexSet := by
            simp only [V, Set.mem_iUnion, Set.mem_setOf_eq] at h₇
            simp_all [Graph.ext_iff]
            <;> aesop
          exact h₈
        -- Therefore, x ∈ G.vertexSet
        exact h₆ hx
      -- Since I ⊆ V(G, M) and V(G, M) ⊆ G.vertexSet, we have I ⊆ G.vertexSet
      exact Set.Subset.trans hI h₁
    exact h_main

intro S hS
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hM'⟩ := hS
    -- We need to show that S ⊆ V(G).
    -- Since S ⊆ V(G, M) and V(G, M) ⊆ V(G), we can use transitivity.
    have h₁ : S ⊆ V(G, M) := hM'
    have h₂ : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G) := by
      intro v hv
      -- For any vertex v in V(G, M), we need to show it is in V(G).
      -- By definition, V(G, M) is a subset of V(G).
      simp_all [Graph.mem_mul, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      <;> aesop
    -- Using transitivity, we have S ⊆ V(G).
    exact Set.Subset.trans h₁ h₂

intro I hI
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hI⟩ := hI
    have h₁ : I ⊆ V(G) := by
      intro x hx
      have h₂ : x ∈ V(G, M) := hI hx
      have h₃ : x ∈ V(G) := by
        have : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G) := by
          intro y hy
          have h₄ : y ∈ V(G) := by
            have h₅ : ∃ (e : β), e ∈ M ∧ (IsIncidence G y e ∨ IsIncidence G e y) := by
              simp_all [V]
              <;> aesop
            obtain ⟨e, he, h_incd⟩ := h₅
            have h₆ : e ∈ E(G) := hM.1 he
            obtain ⟨x', y', hxy'⟩ := E(G) e
            have h₇ : y = x' ∨ y = y' := by
              cases h_incd with
              | inl h_incd =>
                aesop
              | inr h_incd =>
                aesop
            cases h₇ with
            | inl h₇ =>
              simp_all [V]
              <;> aesop
            | inr h₇ =>
              simp_all [V]
              <;> aesop
          exact h₄
        exact this hy
      exact h₃
    exact h₁

intro S hS
    -- We need to show that S is a subset of G.vertexSet regardless of its independence.
    -- However, we can extract the matching M from hS, and then show that V(G, M) is a subset of G.vertexSet.
    -- But actually, S is already a subset of G.vertexSet because V(G, M) is necessarily a subset of G.vertexSet.
    -- A better approach is to notice that for any set M of edges, V(G, M) is always a subset of G.vertexSet.
    -- Hence, if S is a subset of V(G, M), it is also a subset of G.vertexSet.
    simp only [V_subset_G] at hS ⊢
    -- Using the fact that V_subset_G is trivially true, we can directly conclude that S is a subset of G.vertices.
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hS'⟩ := hS
    exact Set.Subset.trans hS' (by simp [V_subset_G])

intro I hI
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hM'⟩ := hI
    have h₁ : I ⊆ G.vertexSet := by
      intro x hx
      have h₂ : x ∈ G.verticesOf M := hM' hx
      have h₃ : x ∈ G.vertexSet := by
        have h₄ : x ∈ T(G, M) := h₂
        have h₅ : T(G, M) ⊆ G.vertexSet := by
          intro y hy
          simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, T(G, M)] at hy ⊢
          rcases hy with ⟨e, he, hy⟩
          have h₆ : e ∈ E(G) := by
            have h₇ : M ⊆ E(G) := hM.subset
            exact h₇ he
          simp_all [vertexSet]
          <;> aesop
        exact h₅ h₄
      exact h₃
    exact h₁
